name: Muffies: They're the Tops
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 375°F (190°C).
2. Mix the dry ingredients in a bowl: flour, sugar, baking powder.
3. In another bowl, whisk together the wet ingredients: milk, eggs, vanilla extract.
4. Pour the wet mixture into the dry mixture and stir until just combined.
5. If using optional add-ins like blueberries or chopped apples, gently fold them into the batter.
6. Line a baking sheet with parchment paper or grease lightly.
7. Spoon the batter onto the prepared baking sheet, spacing each muffie about 2 inches apart.
8. Bake for 10-12 minutes, or until lightly browned and set in the center.
9. Allow to cool on the baking sheet for a few minutes before transferring to a wire rack to cool completely.
10. Serve warm right from the oven. Reheat by wrapping individual muffies in foil and warming gently in a conventional oven if desired.
11. Store any leftover muffies in a single layer on a baking sheet, then freeze. Once frozen, transfer to an airtight plastic bag for longer storage. Thaw as needed.
